justification: A is slightly better overall. On skill_accuracy, A preserves the MCP tool from the resume's skills section which B omits — a real demonstrated skill. On conservatism, A's skill groupings are more precise (separating Frontend from Backend technologies) and better calibrated to the evidence. Both outputs capture concrete metrics faithfully (1,000+ orders, 52% latency reduction, 98% time reduction, ~5 hrs/week saved) giving equal impact_quality scores. Project classification is equivalent across both — neither output uses the taxonomy labels explicitly, but both correctly identify the nature of each project. The github/linkedin URLs in B are truncated/guessed ('sujan' vs the more complete attempt in A), which is a minor accuracy issue. Overall A is the cleaner, more complete extraction.
